retry_reason: Failure (allocation failure or hold failure) indicates either memory pressure or the resource is dying; retrying is unlikely to succeed under the same conditions.
contract_summary: This function creates a capture node that holds two references (one via i915_vma_resource_hold, one via i915_vma_resource_get) on the given VMA resource. On success, the returned capture node is linked into a chain (via next), and the caller owns the entire chain. The caller must eventually release all references by calling the appropriate put functions (e.g., i915_vma_resource_put) on each node. If the hold fails or allocation fails, the function returns the original chain head unchanged; the caller should treat this as a non-fatal capture skip. The capture node stores a copy of the name string for debugging.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to capture a VMA resource by allocating a capture node and acquiring two references (hold and get) on the resource. The allocation and string copy are minimal bookkeeping around the refcount operations.

check.reason: The first operation is supported: i915_vma_resource_hold(vma_res) matches the callee info for hold_count.refs. The second operation lacks callee information for i915_vma_resource_get, so it is removed.
